summary: Tests exercise core requirements (parsing JSON/requirements.txt, license checking with allow/deny/unknown statuses, mocking) but lack error handling coverage. Missing tests for: nonexistent files, malformed manifests, unsupported file types, empty dependencies, complex version specifiers, and mixed-status dependency sets. Design uses proper Pester structure with temp file fixtures and cleanup, but fixtures are fragile (Get-Random naming) and BeforeEach declares unused $script:MockLicenses. No edge case testing severely limits confidence in regression detection; would fail to catch handling of invalid inputs or boundary conditions the implementation attempts to guard against.
